strengths: 20+ years shipping products with deep real-time systems expertise (Dunnhumby: microservices handling millions req/sec)—exactly the technical foundation fintech infrastructure requires
Currently architecting AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, actively building agentic workflow systems
Demonstrated teaching and mentorship (refactoring dojos, code katas, organized CI infrastructure)—suggests leadership aptitude despite lack of formal management title
concerns: No formal team leadership; 20-year individual-contributor career with no demonstrated experience hiring, managing, or scaling engineering organizations
AI/ML production depth limited to recent role with vague characterization—no evidence of designing multi-agent feedback loops, observability infrastructure, or production failure handling at scale
Fintech domain expertise absent; RIDE Capital mention ('reshaping investment practices') lacks specifics on credit systems, payment flows, embedded finance, or regulatory compliance

key_factors.factor: Deep real-time systems and distributed infrastructure expertise
No demonstrated team leadership or organization scaling
Recent, limited multi-agent AI systems production

response.justification: Candidate brings genuine technical excellence—20 years shipping products, expertise in real-time distributed systems (microservices at scale), and current focus on the exact domain required (AI-agent infrastructure). Mentoring and teaching history suggest leadership potential. However, resume shows no formal team leadership experience and AI/ML production work is recent. These gaps (people leadership, multi-agent system design depth, fintech domain knowledge) are material for a CTO but candidate's technical credibility and current architectural positioning could support growth with strong mentorship.
